steps.title: Compute the increase
steps.content: The price went from $\$16$ to $\$80$, an increase of $\$80 - \$16 = \$64$.

steps.title: Convert to percent of original
steps.content:
Percent increase $=\dfrac{\text{increase}}{\text{original}} \times 100\%$:

$$\frac{64}{16} \times 100\% = 4 \times 100\% = 400\%$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
